goal: 用 GitHub Pages 发布 Mainline Hub

summary.title: Publish Mainline Hub through GitHub Pages
summary.what: Added a GitHub Actions workflow that builds the Mainline CLI, syncs Mainline state, exports Hub into a Pages artifact, verifies the export contains intent data, and deploys it through GitHub Pages. README and reference docs now link and explain the hosted Hub path.
summary.why: The generated Hub should be viewable as static HTML without committing generated files or coupling this repo to the separate website app.
summary.user_goal: 用 GitHub Pages 发布 Mainline Hub

decisions.chose: Use this repository's own GitHub Pages workflow as the first hosted Hub path.
decisions.rationale: Hub is generated from this repo's Git refs and notes, so the publishing job should live beside the Git-native state it exports.

decisions.chose: Keep generated Hub files out of git; export to _site and upload a Pages artifact.
decisions.rationale: The Hub is rebuildable state, and committing mainline-hub or hub-export output would add churn without improving review.

decisions.chose: Run the workflow on main updates, manual dispatch, and a daily schedule.
decisions.rationale: Mainline refs and notes can change independently of code diffs, so hosted Hub needs a refresh path beyond push-to-main.

decisions.chose: Document the hosted Hub URL in README while keeping operational details in docs/reference.md and docs/reference.zh.md.
decisions.rationale: README should expose the workflow and link; reference docs carry the why and setup detail.

decisions.chose: Keep repository contents read-only in the workflow; grant only Pages and OIDC permissions for deployment.
decisions.rationale: The job publishes a static artifact and should not need to write repository contents.

rejected.alternative: Commit mainline-hub or hub-export output into the repository.
rejected.reason: Generated Hub output is ignored and rebuildable; committing it would create noisy static-site diffs.

rejected.alternative: Move Hub generation into ../website for this pass.
rejected.reason: The repo-local Pages workflow is simpler and keeps Hub tied to the Git-native Mainline state.
The default mainline-org.github.io/mainline/ URL is enough until DNS is chosen.

review_notes: Verified local export with ./mainline hub export /private/tmp/mainline-hub-pages-verify --json: 228 intents, 258 files, 5 actors.
Verified exported Hub has no root-relative href/src paths, so it should work under the GitHub Pages /mainline/ subpath.
Verified git diff --check and make hygiene pass.
Verified the workflow build command with env GOCACHE=/private/tmp/go-build-mainline go build -o /private/tmp/mainline-hub-pages-mainline .
The workflow itself will only execute after the workflow file is merged to main; repository Pages source still needs GitHub Actions if it is not already configured.

architectural_claims: Hosted Hub is a rebuildable static artifact derived from Mainline Git refs and notes, not a committed build output.
GitHub Pages is the publishing surface for the open repo; the separate website app does not own Hub generation in this pass.
behavioral_changes: Main pushes, manual dispatches, and daily scheduled runs can publish the latest Hub snapshot to GitHub Pages.
README readers now have a direct Hosted Hub link and CLI/Hub documentation explains the Pages workflow.
security_implications: The workflow grants contents: read, pages: write, and id-token: write; it does not request contents write.
migration_notes: Enable GitHub Pages with GitHub Actions as the source before expecting deploy-pages to publish.
Existing local Hub exports remain ignored and should not be committed.
